edge_hypothesis: The ea9p5k2m ACCEPT (PF=1.288, transitional PF=2.765) proved that ema_rsi_atr with narrow RSI bands (50-65 long / 35-50 short) produces actionable edge on ETH/4h. SOL has NEVER been tested by any Claude spec. SOL has 1.5-2x ETH's average volatility, which creates three structural advantages for ema_rsi_atr: (1) ATR-based stops and TPs scale naturally with SOL's larger price swings, so the same R:R ratio captures proportionally larger absolute moves. (2) The ATR expansion gate (ATR > ATR_MA20) becomes MORE selective on SOL because SOL's volatility distribution has fatter tails — the gate rejects average-vol bars and only allows entry when SOL is making a genuine volatility expansion move. (3) SOL trends harder than ETH — when SOL moves, it commits. EMA_9 > EMA_21 trend alignment on SOL should produce higher win rates on the trend-following component. The narrow RSI bands (50-65 long) ensure entries only occur when momentum is confirmed but not exhausted — the 'conviction zone' where price is moving with purpose but hasn't reached overbought territory. Additionally, testing ETH/1h extends the ea9p5k2m thesis to a faster timeframe — 1h provides ~6x more signal opportunities, and if the narrow-band principle holds, PF should remain above 1.2 with 200+ trades for strong statistical confidence. Invalidation: if SOL/4h PF < 1.0, SOL's volatility profile is incompatible with this signal structure; if ETH/1h PF < 1.0, the narrow-band principle is timeframe-specific to 4h.

variants.name: sol_narrow_band_9to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr with proven narrow RSI bands on SOL/4h with 9:1 R:R. Direct transplant of ea9p5k2m's winning formula to SOL. The 9:1 R:R matches ea9p5k2m's ACCEPT configuration exactly. SOL's higher ATR means larger absolute TP distances, capturing SOL's characteristic explosive moves. Narrow RSI 50-65 (long) / 35-50 (short) keeps entries in the momentum conviction zone — not overbought, not mean-reverting, just trending with purpose.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 50 and 65 (momentum confirmed but not exhausted — narrow conviction band).
ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate — SOL is making a decisive move).
entry_short: RSI_14 between 35 and 50 (momentum confirmed but not oversold).
ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 on SOL, the volatility profile is incompatible with narrow-band RSI entries
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 — short-term trend direction on SOL
RSI_14 narrow band 50-65 long / 35-50 short — momentum conviction zone, proven on ETH ea9p5k2m
ATR_14 expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 18.0x TP — 9:1 R:R, SOL's higher vol means more selective gate

variants.name: sol_narrow_band_7to1_conservative
variants.description: Same narrow-band formula on SOL/4h but with 7:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 14.0x TP). A more conservative R:R that should produce more winning trades (TP hit more often) at the cost of lower per-trade profit. If SOL's volatility makes 9:1 TP too distant, 7:1 captures shorter moves. Comparing PF between 7:1 and 9:1 reveals whether SOL's trend extensions match ETH's or are structurally shorter.
entry_long: ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
entry_short: ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if 7:1 PF > 9:1 PF, SOL trend extensions are shorter than ETH — use lower R:R on SOL
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 — trend direction on SOL/4h
RSI_14 narrow band 50-65/35-50 — same conviction zone as ea9p5k2m ACCEPT
ATR_14 expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R, testing if SOL prefers lower R:R than ETH

variants.name: eth_1h_narrow_band_8to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr narrow bands transplanted to ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R. ea9p5k2m was ETH/4h — this tests whether the narrow-band principle scales to 1h where ~6x more bars produce more signal opportunities. On 1h, EMA_9/EMA_21 crossovers happen more frequently but are noisier. The ATR expansion gate becomes critical: only enter when 1h volatility spikes above its 20-bar mean, filtering out low-conviction chop. Expect 200-400 trades vs ea9p5k2m's 92, providing much stronger statistical confidence. 8:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 16.0x TP) is slightly lower than ea9p5k2m's 9:1 to account for 1h's shorter trend legs.
entry_long: EMA_9 > EMA_21 (short-term trend is bullish on 1h bars).
ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion — critical noise filter on 1h).
entry_short: EMA_9 < EMA_21 (short-term trend is bearish on 1h bars).
ATR_14 > 20-period MA of ATR_14 (volatility expansion gate).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < ea9p5k2m's 1.288 on 4h, narrow bands are timeframe-specific and don't scale to 1h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 — trend direction on ETH/1h, faster signal than 4h
RSI_14 narrow band 50-65/35-50 — proven on 4h, testing 1h scalability
ATR_14 expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R, slightly lower than 4h to account for 1h's shorter moves
